title: TC005-Select Boletín 1 from sidebar and open an exercise in the workspace
description: Verifies that selecting 'Boletín 1' loads an exercise list and an exercise can be opened in the workspace.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8080
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /logica/ (http://localhost:8080/logica/) to locate 'Boletín 1' in the sidebar and continue the verification steps.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080/logica/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Boletín 1').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//h2[contains(., "Exercise list")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(., "Workspace")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Boletín 1').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Sidebar entry 'Boletín 1' not found on /logica/ page: no interactive element with that text or identifier was present in the page's interactive elements.
- Exercise list cannot be validated because the prerequisite action (clicking 'Boletín 1') is not possible; there is no accessible target to trigger loading of an exercise list.
- Attempts to locate 'Boletín 1' via scrolling and find_text were performed (1 attempt each) and did not reveal the element.

title: TC008-Selecting Boletín 1 and handling 'Exercise not found' error state
description: Verifies that an explicit error message is shown when exercises cannot be found after selecting 'Boletín 1'.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8080
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /logica/ (use navigate action to http://localhost:8080/logica/ as the test step explicitly requires).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080/logica/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(text(), 'Loading')]").first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Exercise not found').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Boletín 1 sidebar item not found on /logica/ page; element absent prevents performing the required click action.
- Cannot verify 'Loading' indicator because the Boletín 1 selection step could not be completed.
- Cannot verify 'Exercise not found' message because exercises were not requested (selection failed).

title: TC009-Selecting Boletín 1 and handling 'Failed to load exercises' error state
description: Verifies that a failure message is shown when loading exercises fails after selecting 'Boletín 1'.
code:

testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- 'Boletín 1' sidebar item or button not found on page (no interactive element labeled 'Boletín 1' present).
- Cannot select 'Boletín 1', so the exercise-loading flow could not be triggered for verification.
- 'Loading' indicator verification could not be performed because the required selection step was not possible.
- 'Failed to load exercises' message not observed because exercises were never requested/loaded.

title: TC018-Advance to next proof step and verify explanation updates
description: Verifies clicking Next advances the proof step and the explanation panel reflects the new current step.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8080
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /logica/ (explicit navigation step).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080/logica/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Next' button (use interactive element index 1815) to advance the proof step.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/main/section[5]/div/div[2]/div[2]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Explanation panel').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Step').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Clicking the Next button (id=btn-next) did not change the EXPLICACIÓN panel — no 'Step', 'Paso N', numbered step, or new step content is present after the click.
- The EXPLICACIÓN panel content remains only headings (e.g., 'EXPLICACIÓN' followed immediately by 'HIPÓTESIS ACTIVAS') with no visible step text between them.
- A click event for the Next button was recorded, but no visible UI state change in the explanation panel occurred following the click.
- No alternative UI indicator of proof advancement (numbered step, 'Paso', 'Step', or updated explanation text) was found in the extracted page content after the Next click.

title: TC023-Start exercise in Declarative (Isar) mode from the modal
description: Verifies that choosing Modo Declarativa opens the workspace in a declarative view.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8080
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /logica/ to start the exercise flow.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080/logica/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Modo Declarativa').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Workspace').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Isar').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Sidebar exercise list not found on /logica/ page; no clickable topic or exercise items are present.
- 'Start Exercise' button not found on the page and no obvious control to begin an exercise is available.
- 'Modo Declarativa' option is not present as an interactive element on the page and cannot be selected.
- Page contains only informational detail sections and an admin GitHub connection form; exercise selection controls appear to be missing.

title: TC024-Applicative mode shows unavailable error message when selected
description: Verifies that choosing Modo Aplicativa results in a visible unavailable message instead of opening the workspace.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8080
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /logica/ (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:8080/logica/)
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8080/logica/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Modo Aplicativa').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Mode unavailable').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Modo Declarativa').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Sidebar exercise items are not present as interactive elements on the /logica/ page, preventing selection of an exercise.
- No 'Start Exercise' button or link was found on the page to launch an exercise workspace.
- The 'Modo Aplicativa' option could not be clicked because the exercise-launch UI is missing.
- Visibility of 'Mode unavailable' and 'Modo Declarativa' could not be verified because the relevant controls or modal did not appear.
